  • Card Name: On Faction Cards, the name may also serve as a Keyword identifying faction (e.g. Kerrigan's Brood).

  • Type: Clearly labeled as either a Faction Card or a Tactical Card.

  • Resources: The specific resource type (CP, BM, PE) the card provides when Exhausted.

  • Special Abilities: One or more abilities resolved using the standard rules.

  • Uniqueness: If the card has the Unique tag, a player may include only one copy of it in their army.

  • Army Slots: Some cards grant or occupy Army Slots, affecting army building.

5.3.1 RESOURCES & PAYING COSTS

Powerful abilities require resources. These are generated dynamically by your cards:


RESOURCE TYPES

Depending on your army’s faction, costs are paid in specific currencies:

  • Terran: Command Points (CP).

  • Zerg: Biomass (BM).

  • Protoss: Psionic Energy (PE).


EXHAUSTING CARDS 

To pay a cost listed on an ability (e.g. "Cost: 1 CP"), the player must Exhaust a Ready (face-up) Tactical or Faction Card that possesses that resource value:

  • Flip the card to its reverse side, known as the Exhausted side.

  • If a player cannot Exhaust enough cards to pay the full cost, the ability cannot be used.

  • If the card has more resources than is required to Activate the special skill, the remaining resource is lost.

5.3.2 MANAGING CARD STATES

Managing the state of your deck is a core skill. Players must decide when to commit a card and accept that its advantages will be unavailable for the remainder of the round.


METHODS OF EXHAUSTION

A card becomes Exhausted in one of two ways:

  • You Exhaust the card to use a Special Ability printed on the card itself.

  • You Exhaust the card to generate its Resource (CP/BM/PE) to pay for an ability located elsewhere (e.g. on a Unit Card).


EFFECTS OF EXHAUSTION

Once a card is Exhausted (face-down):

  • It provides no further resources and its abilities cannot be used.

  • It remains Exhausted until the Scoring & Cleanup Phase (Phase 4), at which point it is Refreshed (flipped face-up).

Keywords (written in BOLD CAPS) represent standardized effects shared between factions. Each Keyword entry has a Type (Terrain, Weapon Trait, Status, Effect etc.) to highlight the area of the same that it applies to.


Stacking Rules 

Unless explicitly stated otherwise, Keywords and Special Abilities are not cumulative:

  • Duplicates: Gaining the same Keyword twice provides no extra benefit.

  • Numeric Values: If a Unit has Precision (1) and gains Precision (2), the values do not add together. The Unit benefits only from the single instance with the highest value.


ACCESS POINT

Part of a Terrain that connects different elevation levels. Models moving through an ACCESS POINT can change their elevation.A specific type of Terrain (Size 1) that connects different elevation levels. Models may move onto and end their movement atop an ACCESS POINT to traverse between GROUND LEVEL and HIGH GROUND. Additionally, models on an ACCESS POINT can be Engaged with models on both HIGH GROUND and GROUND LEVEL (overriding Section 2.6.1).


ANTI-EVADE (X)

When resolving an attack with this weapon against enemy units, the target unit suffers a -X Modifier to its Evade roll for this attack (modifying Section 4.6.4, Step 4).


BUFF [Characteristic] (X)

The unit gains a bonus of X to the specified Characteristic until the End of the Round.

  • If the Characteristic is a Target Number (e.g. Armour, Hit), reduce the value by X (making the roll easier).

  • If the Characteristic is a value (e.g. Speed, RoA), increase the value by X.

BULKY

This weapon cannot be selected to make an attack if the unit making a Ranged Attack is currently ENGAGED (restricting Section 4.6.3).


BURROWED

  • A unit with the BURROWED status gains the HIDDEN Keyword.

  • While BURROWED, the unit’s Size is treated as 0 and its Current Supply Value is treated as 0 for the purpose of resolving Disengage actions. Units with the BURROWED status cannot Control or Contest Mission Markers (overriding Section 2.2, Step 2 and Section 4.8).

  • While BURROWED, a unit may only perform Move, Run, Disengage, Hold, and Close Ranks actions. Performing any of these actions (except Hold) immediately removes the BURROWED status.

  • Units with the BURROWED status may use Special Abilities, unless explicitly stated otherwise.

  • BURROWED units are eligible to make an Evade roll against every attack targeting them.

  • Models belonging to BURROWED units do not block other models' movement, provided Enemy models do not end their movement within Engagement Range of the BURROWED unit (overriding Section 4.4.2).


BURST FIRE Y” (X)

When making a Ranged Attack, if the target unit is within Y" of the attacking model, increase this weapon’s Rate of Attack (RoA) by X for that attack.


CRITICAL HIT (X)

Immediately after the roll to Hit, move X dice from the Armour Pool directly to the Damage Pool. These dice bypass the Armour Roll entirely. CRITICAL HIT can never move more dice than are currently in the Armour Pool.


DEBUFF [Characteristic] (X)

The unit suffers a penalty of X to the specified Characteristic until the End of the Round.

  • If the Characteristic is a Target Number (e.g., Armour, Hit), increase the value by X (making the roll harder).

  • If the Characteristic is a value (e.g., Speed, RoA), decrease the value by X (to a minimum of 0).

DISPLACEMENT

Leading Model may end a move Overlapping this token (overriding Section 2.4.1).

If a Leading Model ends a Move, Run, Charge, Disengage, Close Ranks action or any Special Ability Overlapping this token:

  • The Controlling Player of the token must immediately place it in Base-to-Base contact with the Leading Model.

  • If strictly Base-to-Base is not possible (due to terrain or other models), the Controlling Player places the token as close as possible to the Leading Model.


DODGE (X)

When this unit is the target of an attack, reduce the total number of dice moved from the Armour Pool directly to the Damage Pool generated by SURGE or CRITICAL HIT by X (to a minimum of 0). This modifier is applied during the Resolve Surge step.


ENTRY EDGE

The specific table edge assigned to a player, as defined by the active Deployment Card. It serves as the primary location from which units perform a Move action to enter the battlefield from Reserves.


HEAL (X)

Remove X points of accumulated Damage from the unit (reduce its marker(s) accordingly).


HIDDEN

A unit with this Keyword cannot be selected as the target of a Ranged Attack, Charge action, or any Special Ability that requires Line of Sight, unless the acting model is within 4" of it.

A Unit with the HIDDEN Keyword is eligible to make an Evade Roll against every attack targeting it.


Hits X (Y)

The affected unit suffers X automatic hits. Place X dice directly into the Armour Pool.

Proceed immediately to Step 6: Defense Rolls (resolving Armour Rolls and Evade Rolls as normal). For the purpose of resolving these hits, the Damage characteristic is treated as Y. These hits do not generate a Surge.

IMPACT HITS (X) Y

Immediately after this unit completes a successful Charge action, resolve this ability.

Check through every model in this unit that is currently in the Fighting Rank or Supporting Rank. For each eligible model, generate X Impact Dice and allocate them as follows:

  • If the model is in the Fighting or Supporting Rank of only one Enemy unit, all X dice must be allocated to that enemy unit.

  • If the model is in the Fighting or Supporting Rank of more than one Enemy unit simultaneously, the Controlling Player may split that model's X dice between those specific Enemy units in any combination.

Once all dice have been allocated, roll them separately for each target unit. For each result of Y or higher, place 1 die directly into the target's Armour Pool.

Proceed immediately to Defense Rolls. These hits do not generate a Surge and treat their Damage characteristic as 1.


INDIRECT FIRE

Ranged Attacks made with this weapon may ignore Line of Sight requirements when selecting a target (the target must still be within Range).

If the selected target is not Visible to the attacking model, the target unit is eligible to make an Evade roll against this attack.


INSTANT

Enemy units cannot declare or resolve Reaction abilities in response to attacks made with this weapon.


LOCKED IN (X)

When making a Ranged Attack with this weapon, add X to its Rate of Attack (RoA) if the target unit has not performed a Move, Disengage, Run, Charge action or PLACE, during the current Game Round.


LONG RANGE (X)

The maximum Range of this weapon is increased to X.

When resolving a Ranged Attack, measure the distance from each attacking model to the target unit individually:

  • Models within the weapon's profile Range resolve their attacks normally.

  • Models beyond the profile Range (but within Range X) suffer a -1 Modifier to their Hit roll (increasing the Target Number by 1).

Note: If a firing unit has models at both Standard and Long Range, generate the total dice for the Batch but roll the two groups separately to account for the different Hit Target Numbers.

NON-LETHAL DAMAGE (X)

The unit suffers X points of Damage. Add this amount effectively to the unit's Damage Marker.


Unlike standard Damage, the application of NON-LETHAL DAMAGE does not trigger the Remove Casualties step (Section 4.6.4, Step 5), even if the unit's Total Damage exceeds its Hit Points (HP) (overriding Section 4.6.4, Step 5).


ON CREEP

A unit is considered to be ON CREEP while it is within 6" of a Creep Tumor token or any Friendly model designated as a Source of Creep.

While satisfying this condition, the unit gains the ON CREEP Keyword. This allows the unit to trigger specific Special Abilities or Upgrades that require this state.


PIERCE [TAG] X

When resolving an attack against a unit possessing the specified Combat Tag (e.g., Armoured, Biological), treat this weapon’s Damage characteristic as X.


PINPOINT

      Ranged Attacks made with this weapon may target engaged Enemy units ignoring the standard Engagement            Status restriction (overriding Section 4.6.3).

PLACE (X)

Select a model in this unit to be the Leading Model. Remove it from the battlefield and set it up again wholly within X" of its starting position.

Then, remove the remaining models of the unit and set them up maintaining unit Coherency (Section 1.6.4).

When placing models via this rule:

• Ignore intervening models and terrain (unless the position itself is impassable).

• Models may be placed within the Engagement Range of Enemy models (the unit becomes Engaged), unless stated otherwise.


PRECISION (X)

When resolving an attack with this weapon, after rolling to Hit, you may move up to X failed Attack Dice directly into the Armour Pool.

These dice are treated as successful Hits for all purposes, including resolving Surge (they may be moved to the Damage Pool if the weapon has a matching Surge Type).


RESPAWN (X)

Return up to X destroyed models to this unit.

  • The return of these models cannot cause the unit’s Current Supply Value to increase. You may not return a model if doing so would push the unit’s model count into a higher bracket on its Supply Profile.

  • Place each returned model in Base-to-Base Contact with an existing model in the unit. Returned models cannot be placed within Engagement Range (1") of any Enemy unit.

Note: If a model cannot be placed legally due to terrain or enemy proximity, it cannot be returned.


REPETITIVE

This ability is exempt from the standard "Once per Round" limit (Section 1.8.2).

The unit may use this ability multiple times during the same Game Round (and even multiple times during the same Activation), provided that all required Costs (such as CP, BM, or PE) and trigger conditions are met for each individual use.

SIDEARM

When this unit performs a Ranged Attack action, models equipped with this weapon may fire it in addition to one other available weapon (overriding Section 4.6.3).

Attacks made with the SIDEARM must be resolved as a separate Batch.

Note: Because they are a separate Batch, Sidearm attacks may target a different Enemy unit than the other weapons fired by the model.


SPECIALIST

A unit may include only one model equipped with a weapon that has the SPECIALIST Keyword. You cannot purchase multiple upgrades or configure a unit to include more than one instance of this weapon (Section 3.1.7).


STAY IN PLAY

This token, marker, or Ability Effect is persistent. It is not removed during the Cleanup & Refresh step of the Scoring & Cleanup Phase (overriding the standard expiration rules in Section 2.4.1 and Section 5.2). It remains in play until a specific condition causes its removal (e.g. it is destroyed or a specific duration expires).


SUMMON (Unit Name)

Place the Leading Model of the Named unit in Base-to-Base Contact with the Parent unit and place all remaining models In Coherency. Models cannot be placed within Engagement Range (1") of any enemy unit.

Requirements:

  • Upon arrival, the unit is immediately considered Activated for the current Phase. Place an Activation Marker next to the Unit.

  • The player must have sufficient Available Supply to place this unit. If the unit’s Supply Cost causes the player's Total Current Supply to exceed their Supply Pool, the unit cannot be Summoned.


TOUGH (X)

When this unit resolves an Armour Roll, you may change up to X failed results into successes.

Treat these dice as if they had met or exceeded the unit's Armour characteristic. They are discarded and do not move to the Damage Pool.

1 COMBAT & FACTION TAGS

In StarCraft, interactions between Units are governed by Tags. These are Keywords found at the bottom of the Unit Card. They allow rules to interact with specific types of Units without repeating full rules text.

1.1 FACTION TAGS (LOGISTICS)

These identify the Unit’s allegiance and origin.

  • Race Tags: TERRAN, ZERG, PROTOSS.

  • Sub-Faction Tags: Specific broods or organizations (e.g. KERRIGAN’S BROOD, RAYNOR’S RAIDERS).

  • Function:

    • Army Building: You may only include Units that share Faction Tags with your chosen Faction Card.

    • Tactical Cards: You may only include Tactical Cards that match your Faction Tags.

2. COMBAT TAGS 

These identify the Unit’s physical nature and tactical class

Type Tags: BIOLOGICAL, MECHANICAL, PSIONIC.

  • Function:

    • Targeting: Weapons may be restricted to specific tags (e.g. "Target: FLYING").

    • Surge: Weapons trigger their Surge efficiency (Automatic Penetration) only if the Target possesses the specific Combat Tag listed in the weapon’s Surge Type (e.g. a weapon with Surge: Light ignores armour against LIGHT Units).

    • Bonuses: Abilities like ANTI-EVADE (X) or PIERCE (X) often apply only against specific tags.

3 PLAYER ROLES & CONTROL

Clear language prevents arguments. The following terms define who makes decisions.

  • ACTIVE PLAYER: The player currently resolving an activation or rule sequence. During a Unit’s activation, its owner is the Active Player.

  • CONTROLLING PLAYER: The player who exercises command over a specific Unit, Model, or Token. They make all decisions regarding movement, targeting, and rolling dice for that element.

  • OPPONENT: Any player on the opposing side. In team games (e.g. 2v2), any member of the opposing team is an Opponent.

  • FRIENDLY: Any element belonging to the controlling player or their teammates.

  • ENEMY: Any element belonging to an Opponent.

  • CHANGE OF CONTROL: If a rule (e.g. Neural Parasite) changes control of a Unit, the new controller becomes the controlling player for the duration of the effect.
